newsSinceJun6: May 2026 revenue reported at NT$290.18B, +39.2% YoY and up ~2.4% MoM vs April's NT$283.4B - a fresh monthly record that keeps the AI-server ramp intact. Headline YoY decelerated sharply from April's +112% purely on base effect (May 2025 had spiked +162% YoY to NT$208.4B). FactSet consensus EPS was revised up to NT$13.71 with a NT$203 mean target (vs NT$165.5 price). No new contracts, M&A, or capacity announcements since June 6; +1.22% day move is unremarkable.

sources.label: cnyes - Wistron May 2026 revenue NT$290.18B, +39.24% YoY
Wistron IR - monthly revenue table (April NT$283.44B, +112% YoY)
DIGITIMES - Wistron Q1 profit triples on server surge, robust AI demand outlook
cnyes/FactSet - consensus EPS raised to NT$13.71, target NT$203

verdicts.rankRationale: May's record NT$290B print confirms the AI-server ramp without triggering a rerating - the stock is only +6.8% YTD against +39% YoY revenue growth, so residual alpha is intact but not improved enough to jump higher-conviction names above it.
verdicts.bull: Revenue compounding at record levels with GB300 rack shipments still ramping into H2, while the share price has barely rerated; consensus target of NT$203 implies ~23% upside on a mid-cap that passives haven't crowded.
verdicts.neutral: Monthly revenue is routine confirmation, not new evidence of bottleneck scarcity; YoY optics decelerate hard from here on 2025 base effects, capping near-term rerating triggers.
verdicts.bear: ODM assembly carries thin margins and customer concentration; if AI server orders pause or pricing compresses, the 79%-server revenue mix becomes a liability rather than leverage.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H2 2026 GB300 rack shipment ramp and Q2 results showing AI server mix margin resilience.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Base-effect YoY deceleration (+39% in May vs +112% in April) being misread as demand cooling, or genuine order digestion at hyperscalers.

verdicts.rankRationale: Rank was already moved up yesterday on deployment-relevance evidence; today's May revenue print is incremental confirmation of rack-integration execution, not genuinely new information warranting a second consecutive move.
verdicts.bull: Record May revenue on top of a record Q1 (revenue +144% YoY, EPS NT$3.06) shows Wistron is converting physical-deployment exposure into actual shipped racks faster than the rerating reflects.
verdicts.neutral: Execution is on track but the print was in line with the trajectory already priced after yesterday's upgrade; nothing changes the deployment-capacity thesis either way.
verdicts.bear: Deployment-shell economics are competitive (Foxconn, Quanta scaling the same GB300 racks); Wistron's edge is execution, not scarcity, so any share loss shows up immediately in monthly revenue.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June/Q2 monthly revenue confirming sequential growth as GB300 transitions from pilot to volume.
verdicts.primaryRisk: ODM competition compressing rack-integration pricing as GB300 supply normalizes across Foxconn/Quanta/Wistron.
